acceptance_targets: [CH14_BLACKOUT_DURATION_CONTINUITY] In the revised sequence spanning snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:17-45, a cold reader can tell that Tomas is still in a full blackout for at least the first ten seconds after onset, or can tell that any eye movement before that is not restored conscious sensation. If the chapter still presents him as having his senses back before Kai's six-second run resolves, this finding remains open.

[CH14_ARCHIVE_DISCOVERY_HANDOUT] In the revised discovery span around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:129-139, the text explicitly states why Kai can access this log now, what he notices that marks the transfer as unauthorized, and why this was not plainly visible in ordinary athlete use before. If he still simply notices a buried admin record with no stated access condition or interpretive hurdle, the beat still fails.

[CH14_YUSUF_EXAMPLE_LINE_ECHO] At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:171 in revision, Yusuf's admission no longer uses the repeated 'I took the data because if I didn't...' cadence or a close paraphrase of that example-line logic. The line must still admit theft and motive, but it must do so in wording that is plainly distinct on first read.

[CH14_COMPOSED_CONFRONTATION_DIALOGUE] In the revised exchange spanning snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:167-243, the scene still clearly lands archive theft, Meridian-signature suspicion, and Tomas's prognosis, but no longer relies on consecutive polished mini-speeches to do it. At least one major disclosure must be interrupted, redirected, or verbally fumbled under pressure; if both men still take turns delivering clean explanatory blocks and quotable moral lines, this finding remains open.

[CH14_KAI_POV_ABSTRACT_DRIFT] In the revised Kai POV spans at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:213-214 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:249-251, the emotional and thematic load is carried by concrete perception or plain judgment rather than abstract thesis phrasing. If the narration still sounds more articulate and conceptually complete than Kai plausibly would in that moment, the passage still fails.

[CH14_NEGATION_SCAFFOLD_TIC] Across the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:19, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:91, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:103-105, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:127, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:199, the chapter may retain one concentrated negation-driven intensifier, but the later passages must use materially different sentence shapes to create pressure. If multiple late scenes still rely on back-to-back 'Not ...' corrections as their main emphasis device, this finding remains open.
[PROSE_WRIST_ACHE_chapter_14] Line 2840 references cold air and wrist pain. Does not contain 'knitted too fast' or 'synthetic repair.'
[READER_RETEACH_chapter_14] Lines 2932-2976 do not re-explain the virus mechanism, the contract terms, or Voss-Kessler syndrome. Each reference assumes the reader's prior knowledge.

[R006] Ch 14's description of Petra arriving at Tomas's crash scene does not reference her hair color.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised sequence spanning snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:17-45, a cold reader can tell that Tomas is still in a full blackout for at least the first ten seconds after onset, or can tell that any eye movement before that is not restored conscious sensation. If the chapter still presents him as having his senses back before Kai's six-second run resolves, this finding remains open.
findings.problem: The crash beat contradicts the established blackout behavior. Tomas loses all sensory input at the seventh hurdle, Kai reaches him in six seconds, and by the time Kai is beside him the text states that the blackout has already ended and Tomas's senses have returned. The continuity sheet defines virus blackouts as 10-60 seconds, so this recovery window is too short for the world rule the chapter is using.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the crash-and-aftermath sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:17, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:23, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:39, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:45 so the event obeys the established blackout window. Either keep Tomas fully blacked out through Kai's first response and Petra's arrival, or recast the apparent responsiveness as nonconscious tracking or reflexive movement rather than returned sensation.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised discovery span around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:129-139, the text explicitly states why Kai can access this log now, what he notices that marks the transfer as unauthorized, and why this was not plainly visible in ordinary athlete use before. If he still simply notices a buried admin record with no stated access condition or interpretive hurdle, the beat still fails.
findings.problem: Kai's discovery of Yusuf's archive reads as a plot handout rather than an earned find. The chapter says the transfer log is buried in an administrative sidebar, but gives no reason an athlete has access to that layer now, no reason the anomaly is visible now rather than in earlier sessions, and no obstacle between glancing at a screen and understanding that Yusuf has been siphoning data for weeks.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the discovery beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:129, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:131-136, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:139 so the access path is explicit and consequential. Give Kai a concrete reason he can see the transfer records now, a specific anomaly that distinguishes the illicit traffic from routine calibration chatter, and a brief obstacle or inference step before he can accuse Yusuf.

findings.acceptance_test: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:171 in revision, Yusuf's admission no longer uses the repeated 'I took the data because if I didn't...' cadence or a close paraphrase of that example-line logic. The line must still admit theft and motive, but it must do so in wording that is plainly distinct on first read.
findings.problem: Yusuf's confession line is a near-echo of his calibration-only style-bible example line about taking the data because otherwise nobody else would have it. Because this lands inside the chapter's central trust fracture, the reuse damages voice integrity instead of merely feeling familiar.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the admission at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:171 with materially different syntax, pressure, and justification order. Keep the same underlying facts - he stole the data, he did it for access and research, he is not pretending innocence - but make the sentence sound like this specific crisis, not like reused calibration text.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised exchange spanning snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:167-243, the scene still clearly lands archive theft, Meridian-signature suspicion, and Tomas's prognosis, but no longer relies on consecutive polished mini-speeches to do it. At least one major disclosure must be interrupted, redirected, or verbally fumbled under pressure; if both men still take turns delivering clean explanatory blocks and quotable moral lines, this finding remains open.
findings.problem: The Kai-Yusuf confrontation repeatedly tips into composed dialogue: thesis speeches, balanced explanatory runs, and a mic-drop moral diagnosis instead of messy pressured speech. Yusuf delivers long polished briefings about code provenance and Meridian's strategy; Kai answers with clean rhetorical escalations. The information is legible, but the scene sounds written-about rather than spoken-through.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the confrontation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:167-171, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:185-211, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:237-243. Break at least one major explanation with interruption or failed wording, let Yusuf's private stressed register fracture the professional exposition sooner, and let Kai's anger arrive less as finished diagnosis and more as incomplete, bodily, character-true pressure.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised Kai POV spans at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:213-214 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:249-251, the emotional and thematic load is carried by concrete perception or plain judgment rather than abstract thesis phrasing. If the narration still sounds more articulate and conceptually complete than Kai plausibly would in that moment, the passage still fails.
findings.problem: Kai's close-third narration drifts into abstract thematic language that sounds more essayistic than he should under pressure. Phrases like the conversation being 'about trust' in pure summary and the 'entire apparatus of optimization' read smarter and more self-conscious than Kai's style-bible register, which should stay concrete, slightly indirect, and bodily rather than philosophically articulated.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the interior summary at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:213-214 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:249-251 into perceptions, bodily reactions, or simpler judgments Kai would actually have in the room. Keep the distrust and systemic disgust, but let them emerge through what he sees, what his injured body remembers, what he cannot evaluate on the screens, or what he refuses to say, rather than through thematic abstraction.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:19, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:91, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:103-105, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:127, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:199, the chapter may retain one concentrated negation-driven intensifier, but the later passages must use materially different sentence shapes to create pressure. If multiple late scenes still rely on back-to-back 'Not ...' corrections as their main emphasis device, this finding remains open.
findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same emphatic negation scaffold often enough to feel mechanical: 'Not X. Not Y.' followed by correction or reinterpretation appears in Tomas's blackout, Margot's feed loss, Margot's spinal-sensation description, and Kai's calibration-bay perceptions. A device that works once starts reading as autopilot when it keeps arriving as the chapter's default intensifier.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence architecture at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:19, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:91, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:103-105, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:127, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:199. Keep one negation-driven sequence if you want its force, but make the later shocks land through different syntax and fresh positive physical detail instead of repeating the same denial-and-correction rhythm.

findings.acceptance_test: Line 2840 references cold air and wrist pain. Does not contain 'knitted too fast' or 'synthetic repair.'

evidence.excerpt: 2840: He was standing at the edge of the curve, toweling sweat off his neck, his left wrist aching the way it always ached now — the bone knitted too fast, the synthetic repair leaving a seam in the marrow that the cold indoor air found and pressed on. He'd been watching Tomas without particular attention, the way you watch someone young do something well — appreciating the form, the economy of effort, the eagerness that hadn't been beaten out of him yet.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 2840: He was standing at the edge of the curve, toweling sweat off his neck, his left wrist aching the way it always ached now — the bone knitted too fast, the synthetic repair leaving a seam in the marrow that the cold indoor air found and pressed on. He'd been watching Tomas without particular attention, the way you watch someone young do something well — appreciating the form, the economy of effort, the eagerness that hadn't been beaten out of him yet.

findings.problem: Fourth restatement: 'the bone knitted too fast, the synthetic repair leaving a seam in the marrow that the cold indoor air found and pressed on.' The cold-air detail is scene-specific and fresh; the backstory clause is not.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2840, keep the cold-air detail ('the cold indoor air found and pressed on the marrow') and cut the backstory clause. The reader knows why the wrist hurts.

findings.acceptance_test: Lines 2932-2976 do not re-explain the virus mechanism, the contract terms, or Voss-Kessler syndrome. Each reference assumes the reader's prior knowledge.

evidence.excerpt: 2932: Kai went to check his own system logs because the alternative was standing in the medical bay corridor looking at the closed door behind which Petra and her team were imaging Tomas's spine, and he couldn't do that anymore. He'd stood there for ninety minutes already, his wrist aching, his shoulder aching, his body remembering its own crash with a precision that felt less like memory and more like rehearsal. Tomas's blackout was the same virus that had taken Kai's senses at the 140-meter mark five weeks ago. The same corruption in the shared calibration network. The same firmware eating through the infrastructure that kept their bodies from killing them.

2936: The calibration bay hummed. The sound was always there, the low-frequency drone of processing equipment that Kai had learned to tune out in his first week at the facility. Tonight it sounded different. Not louder. More present. As if the machines were doing something they shouldn't be.

2976: "My sister Farah has Voss-Kessler syndrome," Yusuf said. "Progressive neural deterioration. There's no treatment. There's barely any research, because the condition's rare enough that nobody funds it. But the biological data from enhanced athletes — the neural integration telemetry, the graft interface metrics, the way your systems interact with your native tissue — some of that data maps onto pathways that are relevant to Voss-Kessler. Not directly. It's adjacent. But it's the most detailed neural-interface data set in the world, and it's being generated right here, every day, in every calibration session."

rewrite_direction.excerpt:
2932: Kai went to check his own system logs because the alternative was standing in the medical bay corridor looking at the closed door behind which Petra and her team were imaging Tomas's spine, and he couldn't do that anymore. He'd stood there for ninety minutes already, his wrist aching, his shoulder aching, his body remembering its own crash with a precision that felt less like memory and more like rehearsal. Tomas's blackout was the same virus that had taken Kai's senses at the 140-meter mark five weeks ago. The same corruption in the shared calibration network. The same firmware eating through the infrastructure that kept their bodies from killing them.
2933: 
2934: Tomas had signed Meridian's contract. Had received their patch. The corporate fix — the one that required permanent data surrender, the one that was supposed to be the solution — had not stopped the virus from reaching him.
2935: 
2936: The calibration bay hummed. The sound was always there, the low-frequency drone of processing equipment that Kai had learned to tune out in his first week at the facility. Tonight it sounded different. Not louder. More present. As if the machines were doing something they shouldn't...

findings.problem: Ch 14 re-explains: the virus's relationship to the calibration network (line 2932, already established in Ch 5-6), Meridian's contract terms (line 2936, established in Ch 8), and Yusuf's Farah backstory (line 2976, revealed in Ch 3 and 12). Each of these has been established in prior chapters and is being re-taught to the reader in Kai's POV.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2932-2936, trust the reader to remember the virus and the contract. Replace explanatory clauses with Kai's lived reaction: 'The corporate fix had not stopped the virus from reaching him' conveys the same information without re-teaching the contract terms. At line 2976, Yusuf can reference 'my sister' without re-explaining the syndrome — Kai was in the room when Yusuf told the group in Ch 12.

findings.acceptance_test: Line 2868 does not use 'flat.' Tomas's vocal quality is described in language consistent with his character profile.

evidence.excerpt: 2868: "I can hear you." His voice was still flat. "My legs, Dr. Engel. I can't feel them."

3048: "Don't." Kai's voice was flat. The humor was gone, completely, the way it went when the things he was feeling exceeded the register of irony. "Don't make this about Farah right now. A kid is paralyzed. The corporate patch that was supposed to protect him didn't work. And you've been sitting on virus analysis that suggests Meridian engineered the thing that destroyed his legs. You've been sitting on it."

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 2868: "I can hear you." His voice was still flat. "My legs, Dr. Engel. I can't feel them."

findings.problem: 'His voice was still flat' (Tomas, 2868) and 'Kai's voice was flat' (3048) — two different characters described with identical 'voice was flat' construction in the same chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2868, Tomas's post-crash voice should be described through his specific register — the style bible notes he gets quieter and his English narrows. Replace 'flat' with something that captures his particular version of emptiness: 'His voice was careful' or 'His voice was the voice of minor confusion.' At 3048, keep Kai's 'flat' — it matches his style-bible stress tell.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 14's description of Petra arriving at Tomas's crash scene does not reference her hair color.
findings.evidence: Ch 14 line 2864: 'her gray-streaked hair escaping its pin.'; Established Ch 4 line 942; re-described Ch 7 line 1661.
findings.problem: Third re-description of Petra's grey-streaked hair. The Ch 14 context is an emergency medical response to Tomas's crash; hair color is the least salient detail.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace with an action-focused descriptor — Petra's competence and speed matter here, not her hair color.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 14 contains no more than 10 prose lines with em-dashes (≤~8% of 123 prose lines).
findings.evidence: Ch 14: 57/123 prose lines contain em-dashes = 46.3%. Manuscript-wide: 654/1876 = 34.9% (threshold 10%, target <8%).
findings.problem: Ch 14 has em-dash density of 46.3%, far exceeding the 10% threshold. The em-dash is used for parenthetical asides, appositives, and mid-sentence pivots at a rate that flattens the prose rhythm. Manuscript target: reduce from 654 to <150 em-dash lines.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 14 from 57 to ~10 (47 cuts). Convert parenthetical em-dash asides to commas, semicolons, colons, periods, or restructured sentences. Reserve em-dashes for their highest-impact uses: dramatic interruptions, voice-specific rhythms, and moments where the break creates meaning.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 14 contains no more than 7 instances of 'something.'
findings.evidence: Ch 14: 'something' appears 18 times in 5882 words = 1 per 327 words. Manuscript-wide: 190 occurrences in 78,027 words = 1 per 411 words (threshold: 1 per 800).
findings.problem: 'Something' appears 18 times in Ch 14, a rate of 1 per 327 words. This vague pronoun substitutes for specific imagery. Manuscript-wide target: reduce from 190 to ~98 occurrences.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'something' in Ch 14 from 18 to ~7 (11 cuts). Replace each cut with the specific noun, sensation, or concept the 'something' stands in for. Keep 'something' only where vagueness is the point (character genuinely cannot name the experience).

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 14 contains no more than 7 instances of 'the way.'
findings.evidence: Ch 14: 'the way' appears 15 times in 5882 words = 1 per 392 words. Manuscript-wide: 130 occurrences in 78,027 words = 1 per 600 words (threshold: 1 per 800).
findings.problem: 'The way' appears 15 times in Ch 14, a rate of 1 per 392 words. This construction ('the way she said most things,' 'the way you'd reach for a phone') becomes a habitual sentence shape rather than a deliberate rhetorical choice. Manuscript-wide target: reduce from 130 to ~98.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'the way' in Ch 14 from 15 to ~7 (8 cuts). Replace with direct comparison, metaphor, or restructured syntax. Keep 'the way' only where the simile structure is genuinely the most effective formulation.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
